canonical_name: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
description: A crew of Italian-American mobsters operating in Northern New Jersey, part of the Lucchese crime family. Founded in the 1920s, it was led by Anthony Accetturo and Michael Taccetta during the 1970s-1980s. A war with New York members in the late 1980s led to Accetturo becoming a government witness. Currently controlled by Joseph R. Perna.

country_links.evidence_quote: Accetturo also had Taccetta control his multi-million dollar narcotics distribution network that was operating from South America and the Caribbean to Florida, New Jersey and New York City.
country_links.context: The organization's narcotics distribution network operated from the Caribbean to Florida, New Jersey, and New York City.

country_links.evidence_quote: Accetturo also had Taccetta control his multi-million dollar narcotics distribution network that was operating from South America and the Caribbean to Florida, New Jersey and New York City.
country_links.context: The organization's narcotics distribution network operated from South America to Florida, New Jersey, and New York City.

country_links.evidence_quote: The New Jersey faction of the Lucchese crime family ... operates throughout the Northern New Jersey area.
country_links.context: The organization operates throughout Northern New Jersey and has operations in Florida, New York, and Philadelphia.
